title: Objaverse: A Universe of Annotated 3D Objects
abstract: We present Objaverse 1.0, a large dataset of objects with 800K+ 3D models with descriptive captions, tags, and animations. Objaverse improves upon present day 3D repositories in terms of scale, quality, and diversity.
method_summary: Introduces Objaverse: 800K+ annotated 3D objects from Sketchfab with captions, tags, and animations. Covers diverse categories far beyond CAD-specific datasets. Enables training generative 3D models, improving tail-category segmentation, and open-vocabulary object detection. Foundational dataset for 3D vision research, widely used as pretraining source for CAD-adjacent tasks.

title: Objaverse-XL: A Universe of 10M+ 3D Objects
abstract: We present Objaverse-XL, a dataset of over 10 million 3D objects compiled from diverse sources including manually designed objects, photogrammetry captures, and AI-generated models.
method_summary: Introduces Objaverse-XL: 10M+ 3D objects from GitHub, Thingiverse, Sketchfab, Smithsonian, and polycam. 12x larger than Objaverse 1.0 and 100x larger than all other 3D datasets combined. Demonstrates improved zero-shot generalization in models like Zero123. The scale enables foundation model pretraining for 3D understanding and generation.

title: ShapeNet: An Information-Rich 3D Model Repository
abstract: ShapeNet contains 3D models from a multitude of semantic categories organized under the WordNet taxonomy. It is a collection of datasets providing many semantic annotations for each 3D model such as consistent rigid alignments, parts and bilateral symmetry planes, physical sizes, and keywords.
method_summary: Introduces ShapeNet: seminal 3D model repository with 51K+ models across 55 categories organized by WordNet taxonomy. Provides rich annotations (part segmentation, symmetry, physical size). ShapeNetCore (51K models, 55 categories) and ShapeNetSem (12K models with material/weight annotations). The most widely cited 3D shape dataset, foundational benchmark for shape classification, segmentation, and generation.

title: 3D ShapeNets: A Deep Representation for Volumetric Shapes
abstract: We propose 3D ShapeNets to represent a geometric 3D shape as a probability distribution of binary variables on a 3D voxel grid, using a Convolutional Deep Belief Network. We also introduce ModelNet, a large-scale 3D CAD model dataset.
method_summary: Introduces ModelNet: 127,915 3D CAD models across 662 categories (ModelNet40 subset: 12,311 models, 40 categories). Voxelized representation for deep learning. ModelNet40 became the most widely used benchmark for 3D point cloud classification. Despite being synthetic CAD models, it established the standard evaluation protocol for 3D shape understanding.

title: JoinABLe: Learning Bottom-up Assembly of Parametric CAD Joints
abstract: We introduce JoinABLe, a learning-based method that assembles parts together to form joints. To support future research we release the Fusion 360 Gallery assembly dataset, containing assemblies with rich information on joints, contact surfaces, holes, and the underlying assembly graph.
method_summary: Introduces Fusion 360 Gallery Assembly Dataset: multi-part CAD assemblies with rich joint information (contact surfaces, holes, assembly graphs). Extends the original Fusion 360 Gallery from single-part sequences to multi-part assemblies. Proposes JoinABLe for bottom-up joint prediction. Key benchmark for CAD assembly understanding and generation.

title: AutoMate: A Dataset and Learning Approach for Automatic Mating of CAD Assemblies
abstract: We present a dataset and learning approach for the automatic mating of CAD assemblies. CAD assembly modeling defines assemblies as a system of pairwise constraints, called mates, between parts defined relative to B-rep topology.
method_summary: Introduces AutoMate dataset: CAD assemblies with pairwise mate constraints defined on B-rep topology. Captures how parts connect in real engineering assemblies. Proposes learning-based mate prediction from B-rep features. Addresses the assembly-level understanding gap in CAD datasets, complementing single-part datasets like DeepCAD.

title: ArtiCAD: Articulated CAD Assembly Design via Multi-Agent Code Generation
abstract: We propose ArtiCAD, the first training-free multi-agent system capable of generating editable, articulated CAD assemblies directly from text or images. Our system divides this complex task among specialized agents for part decomposition, geometry generation, and assembly with articulation.
method_summary: Introduces ArtiCAD: first multi-agent system for articulated CAD assembly generation from text/images. Specialized agents handle part decomposition, geometry generation, and assembly with DOF constraints. Training-free approach using LLM agents with CadQuery. Introduces benchmark for evaluating articulated assembly generation quality.

title: CADSmith: Multi-Agent CAD Generation with Programmatic Geometric Validation
abstract: We present CADSmith, a multi-agent pipeline that generates CadQuery code from natural language. It undergoes iterative refinement through two nested correction loops: an inner loop for programmatic geometric validation and an outer loop for visual feedback.
method_summary: Introduces CADSmith: multi-agent text-to-CAD pipeline with dual correction loops. Inner loop uses programmatic geometric validation (dimensional checks, volume verification) rather than lossy visual feedback. Outer loop provides visual refinement. Demonstrates that programmatic validation resolves dimensional errors that visual feedback cannot detect. Advances the agentic CAD generation paradigm.

title: CADgpt: Harnessing Natural Language Processing for 3D Modelling to Enhance Computer-Aided Design Workflows
abstract: This paper introduces CADgpt, an innovative plugin integrating Natural Language Processing with Rhino3D for enhancing 3D modelling in computer-aided design environments.
method_summary: Introduces CADgpt: NLP plugin for Rhino3D that translates natural language into Grasshopper scripts and Rhino commands. Demonstrates practical integration of LLMs into existing commercial CAD software. Addresses the gap between research prototypes and usable CAD tools. One of the first NLP-to-CAD plugins for a production CAD platform.

title: A Survey of AI Methods for Geometry Preparation and Mesh Generation in Engineering Simulation
abstract: Artificial intelligence is beginning to reduce the manual effort in the CAD-to-mesh pipeline. Written for meshing and geometry practitioners with limited AI background, this survey organizes recent work across geometry cleanup, defeaturing, decomposition, and mesh generation.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of AI methods for the CAD-to-simulation pipeline: geometry cleanup, defeaturing, decomposition, and mesh generation. Covers unstructured/block-structured meshing, volumetric parameterizations, and parallel mesh generation. Written for practitioners. Bridges the gap between CAD design tools and downstream engineering simulation.

title: Is Academia Catching Up with Industry Demands? AI for CAE User Experience -- A Multivocal Literature Review
abstract: Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE) enables simulation experts to optimize complex models, but faces challenges in user experience (UX) that limit efficiency and accessibility. While artificial intelligence (AI) offers promising solutions, a gap persists between academic research and industry needs.
method_summary: Multivocal literature review analyzing the gap between academic AI research and industry CAE needs. Identifies UX challenges in simulation tools: steep learning curves, manual preprocessing, and limited accessibility. Maps AI solutions (NLP interfaces, automated meshing, intelligent defaults) to practitioner pain points. Highlights the deployment gap for AI in engineering simulation.

title: FlexCAD: Unified and Versatile Controllable CAD Generation with Fine-tuned Large Language Models
abstract: To achieve controllable generation across all CAD construction hierarchies, such as sketch-extrusion, extrusion, sketch, face, loop and curve, we propose FlexCAD, a unified model by fine-tuning large language models.
method_summary: Proposes FlexCAD: unified controllable CAD generation via LLM fine-tuning with hierarchy-aware masking. Represents CAD as structured text with hierarchical tokens. Enables editing at any construction level (sketch, extrusion, face, loop, curve). Released by Microsoft. Demonstrates that LLMs can serve as versatile CAD editing engines, not just generators.

title: NURBGen: High-Fidelity Text-to-CAD Generation through LLM-Driven NURBS Modeling
abstract: Generating editable 3D CAD models from natural language remains challenging, as existing text-to-CAD systems either produce meshes or rely on scarce design-history data. We present NURBGen, the first system to generate NURBS-based CAD models directly from text using LLMs.
method_summary: Introduces NURBGen: first text-to-NURBS CAD generation system via LLMs. Bypasses the sketch-and-extrude paradigm by directly generating NURBS surface representations. Produces industry-standard B-rep models without requiring design history data. Demonstrates higher geometric fidelity and dimensional accuracy than mesh-based or parametric-sequence approaches.

title: AI-Assisted Analysis and Synthesis of Engineering Systems from Multimodal Engineering Data
abstract: The workbench facilitates an AI-assisted workflow where a designer can ingest source documents, from which the system automatically extracts requirements and uses vision-language models to generate an initial CAD design.
method_summary: Proposes AI-assisted engineering workbench: ingests multimodal engineering documents (specs, drawings, requirements), extracts structured requirements via NLP, and generates initial CAD designs using VLMs. End-to-end workflow from document to design. Demonstrates practical AI integration into engineering systems engineering processes.

title: The Power of Small LLMs in Geometry Generation for Physical Simulations
abstract: Engineers widely rely on simulation platforms like COMSOL or ANSYS to model and optimise processes. However, setting up such simulations requires expertise in defining geometry, generating meshes, establishing boundary conditions. We show that small LLMs can effectively generate simulation geometries.
method_summary: Demonstrates that small LLMs (7B-13B parameters) can generate simulation-ready geometries for COMSOL/ANSYS. Addresses the bottleneck of manual geometry setup in engineering simulation. Shows that domain-specific fine-tuning of small models can match larger models for structured geometry generation. Practical tool for democratizing simulation access.

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: This work introduces CAD-GPT, a CAD synthesis method with spatial reasoning-enhanced multimodal LLM that takes either a single image or a textual description as input and generates parametric CAD construction sequences.
method_summary: Proposes CAD-GPT: spatial reasoning-enhanced multimodal LLM for CAD construction sequence synthesis from images or text. Introduces 3D modeling-specific spatial reasoning capabilities into MLLMs. Accepted at AAAI 2025. Demonstrates that spatial reasoning enhancement is critical for accurate CAD generation from visual inputs.

title: Advancing 3D Generation Evaluation with Hierarchical Validity
abstract: We propose a hierarchical evaluation framework for 3D generation that assesses validity at multiple levels: geometric validity, structural coherence, and semantic alignment.
method_summary: Proposes hierarchical validity framework for 3D generation evaluation: geometric validity (watertight, manifold), structural coherence (part relationships), and semantic alignment (text-shape correspondence). Addresses the limitation of flat metrics (CD, FID) that miss structural quality. Applicable to CAD generation evaluation where structural validity is critical.

title: Objaverse++: Curated 3D Object Dataset with Quality Annotations
abstract: This paper presents Objaverse++, a curated subset of Objaverse enhanced with detailed attribute annotations. Recent advances in 3D content generation have been driven by large-scale datasets such as Objaverse, but quality issues limit their utility for training.
method_summary: Introduces Objaverse++: quality-curated subset of Objaverse with detailed attribute annotations (geometry quality, texture quality, category labels). Addresses the noise and quality variance in large-scale 3D datasets. Demonstrates that curated subsets significantly improve downstream generation quality. Relevant to CAD dataset curation methodology.

title: Scaling Multi-modal CAD Generation using Differentiable Parametric Surfaces
abstract: We propose a scalable approach to multi-modal CAD generation using differentiable parametric surfaces, enabling gradient-based optimization of CAD models conditioned on text, images, or point clouds.
method_summary: Proposes differentiable parametric surface representation for scalable multi-modal CAD generation. Enables gradient-based optimization of CAD models from text/image/point cloud inputs. Bridges the gap between neural implicit representations and explicit parametric CAD. Addresses scalability limitations of autoregressive CAD sequence generation.

title: Towards High-Fidelity CAD Generation via LLM-Driven Program Generation and Text-Based B-Rep Primitive Grounding
abstract: Our method generates executable CadQuery scripts and introduces a text-based query mechanism that enables the LLM to specify geometric selections via natural language, which BRepGround then grounds to actual B-Rep primitives.
method_summary: Introduces BRepGround: text-based B-Rep primitive grounding for LLM-driven CAD generation. LLM specifies geometric selections in natural language, grounded to actual B-Rep topology. Enables precise geometric operations (fillets, chamfers) on specific edges/faces. Addresses the critical gap between LLM text output and precise CAD geometric operations.

title: Synthetic Generation Tool of Digital Measurement Device CAD Model Datasets for Fine-tuning Large Vision-Language Models
abstract: We present a synthetic generation tool for creating CAD model datasets of digital measurement devices, designed for fine-tuning large vision-language models for industrial inspection and quality control applications.
method_summary: Proposes domain-specific synthetic CAD data generation tool for measurement devices. Generates parametrically varied CAD models with annotations for VLM fine-tuning. Targets industrial inspection and quality control. Demonstrates the paradigm of synthetic CAD data generation for specialized manufacturing domains.

title: CADReasoner: Iterative Program Editing for CAD Reverse Engineering
abstract: We propose CADReasoner, an iterative program editing approach for CAD reverse engineering. Across DeepCAD, Fusion 360, and MCB benchmarks, CADReasoner attains state-of-the-art results on clean and scan-sim tracks.
method_summary: Proposes CADReasoner: iterative program editing for CAD reverse engineering from 3D inputs. Achieves SOTA on DeepCAD, Fusion 360, and MCB benchmarks. Introduces edit-based refinement rather than single-pass generation. Demonstrates the value of iterative reasoning for CAD program synthesis, establishing new benchmark baselines.

title: QueryCAD: Grounded Question Answering for CAD Models
abstract: QueryCAD incorporates SegCAD, an open-vocabulary instance segmentation model to identify and select specific parts of the CAD model based on part descriptions. We further propose a CAD question answering benchmark.
method_summary: Introduces QueryCAD: grounded QA system for CAD models with open-vocabulary part segmentation (SegCAD). Proposes first CAD QA benchmark for evaluating natural language understanding of 3D CAD models. Enables users to query specific parts, dimensions, and relationships in CAD models via natural language. New evaluation paradigm for CAD understanding beyond generation.

title: Physics-Grounded Articulation for Simulation-Ready Digital Twins
abstract: Converting static 3D meshes into interactable articulated assets is crucial for embodied AI and robotic simulation. We propose physics-grounded articulation that produces simulation-ready digital twins with accurate joint parameters.
method_summary: Proposes physics-grounded articulation for converting static 3D meshes into simulation-ready digital twins. Automatically infers joint types, axes, and limits from geometry. Produces assets compatible with standard simulators. Bridges the gap between CAD/3D models and interactive simulation environments for robotics and embodied AI.

title: Text2CAD: Text to 3D CAD Generation via Technical Drawings
abstract: Our approach directly translates the user's textual descriptions into detailed isometric images, which are then precisely converted into orthographic views providing sufficient information for 3D CAD model reconstruction.
method_summary: Proposes text-to-CAD via intermediate technical drawings: generates isometric views from text, converts to orthographic projections, then reconstructs 3D CAD models. Novel two-stage pipeline leveraging engineering drawing conventions. Demonstrates that technical drawings serve as an effective intermediate representation between natural language and parametric CAD.

title: Seek-CAD: A Self-refined Generative Modeling for 3D Parametric CAD Using Local Inference via DeepSeek
abstract: Seek-CAD is the pioneer exploration of locally deployed open-source inference LLM DeepSeek-R1 for CAD parametric model generation with a training-free methodology.
method_summary: Introduces Seek-CAD: first exploration of locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 for training-free CAD generation. Demonstrates that reasoning-enhanced open-source LLMs can generate parametric CAD without fine-tuning. Self-refinement loop improves output quality iteratively. Significant for showing that local, private LLM deployment is viable for CAD generation in enterprise settings.
